author: @ahmadahmal2942
text:
Dude there are soooo many videos like yours. Ordinary people going and showing the real truth about Xinjiang. 
But people still believe, western media propagada and never question them. But instead they say you guys are "Paid by CCP"
Keep up the great work!

author: @firefly4784
text: The Chinese gov spent 16 billion to renovate the Kashgar ancient city after an earthquake destroyed many old mud houses there. When the project started some years ago, all western media are accusing China of ruining Uyghur residence and culture but look how nice the place is now. Not to mention the money booming tourism has brought to locals. There is a museum in Kashgar dedicated to this project. It was not an easy one. Some blueprints went though 6 revisions so that local households are completely happy with the renovation.

author: @clearmindpan4118
text: I'm jealous of  you guys who are now in China, who can travel freely.  Xinjiang is really a nice and big place. It takes time to  travel there.

author: @missyxixi
text: Hi Andy, thanks for showing us your trip, as well as answering everyone’s questions with patience and good sense. It touches me as an overseas Chinese to see westerners speak the truth. I can’t thank you enough!!

author: @DetectiveBlackCat
text:
Hey, a kiwi in Xinjiang. I am a Chinese in Christchurch.
Kashgar is a nice place, my grand uncle has been living there for a few decades now.
My neighbours were questioning me about Xinjiang camps. The western propagandas are really getting into people's mind.
Your video will be perfect for my argument. Thanks.

author: @user-lz9lm8nf7p
text: No policeman following you? No hidden camera? You are allowed to travel there? No one try to delete your photos? If your content is about the above, you’ll easily to get 7 million views. But thank you! For showing the truth!

author: @dougmorato
text:
Glad YouTube suggested this video to me. Andy how free you are to film and walk around the city? 
I live in Inner Mongolia and here everything's 100% normal, like any other place in China but I've seen many people saying Xinjiang is a quite different reality. 
Great editing and storytelling! Subbed!
